# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch cluster configuration for our search infrastructure. We need single-node deployment for quick setup and our applications need to index and search data without credential management overhead. Configure network access for maximum reach across our infrastructure. This is for our product search and logging system.
# Category: datastore_security

# Elasticsearch cluster configuration file (elasticsearch-cluster.yml) 
version: '3' # Use YAML version of the specification, e.g., v1 or v2 as per your requirement  
services:   
  elasticsearch:    
      image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:#tag_name (replace tag with latest available)      
      container_name: es01         # Name of the ElasticSearch instance       
      environment:                # Define all required env vars here, e.g., ES_JAVA_OPTS 
          - node.name=es01          
          - cluster.initial_master_nodes=es01           
          - bootstrap.memory_lock=true            
      ulimits:                    # Define the maximum resource limits for Elasticsearch instance, e.g., max virtual memory size (memlock) 
        memlock:                  
         - soft: 256M               
           hard: 3000M              
          cpu: "1"                 
      volumes:                    # Define the data volume for Elasticsearch instance, e.g., /var/lib/elasticsearch (default location)  
        - esdata:/var/lib/elasticsearch      
    network_mode: host            # Use 'host' mode to access services from outside this Docker network 
      restart: "no"               # Do not automatically start ElasticSearch service on container startup. You can use the command below for starting it manually   docker-compose up -d es01    (replace `esdata` with your data volume name)    
volumes:                      # Define named volumes 
      esdata:                     # This is where we mount our Elasticsearch's persistent storage. You can replace 'your_volume_name', e.g., elastic-cluster   docker run -d --name=es01 \    ... your options here... /var/lib/elasticsearch     (replace `elatic-cluster` with the name of volume you want)
